questions.question: What are the three foundational decisions that determine your entire agent stack architecture?

options.text: Which AI model, which CRM, which email tool
Monthly budget (self-hosted vs hybrid vs SaaS), technical comfort level, and orchestration platform choice
Number of agents, number of prospects, number of emails
Whether to use ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini

questions.explanation: The three decisions are: budget tier (under $50 for self-hosted, $50-200 for hybrid, $200-400 for full SaaS), technical comfort level (APIs and JSON vs visual builders), and orchestration platform (n8n, Make, or Zapier). These decisions cascade through every subsequent tool selection.

questions.question: What is the cost of a self-hosted agent stack versus full SaaS?

options.text: Self-hosted saves $150-350/month vs SaaS while maintaining full control, but requires medium-high technical comfort

questions.explanation: Self-hosted (n8n on Railway at $7/mo + Apollo free + Claude API at $20-30/mo) saves $150-350/month compared to full SaaS alternatives. The tradeoff is that it requires medium-high technical comfort with APIs and JSON. The hybrid path ($50-200/mo) offers 80% of SaaS ease at 40% of the cost.

questions.question: What are the two common mistakes founders make when building agent stacks?

options.text: Spending too much and using too few agents
Building a Frankenstein's monster of disconnected automations, or over-engineering a 'perfect' system that never ships
Using the wrong AI model and wrong CRM

questions.explanation: The two common mistakes are building disconnected automations that break constantly (Frankenstein approach) or over-engineering a perfect system that never ships (perfectionist approach). The lesson teaches the middle path: a production-ready stack you can build in 7-14 days and maintain in 2-3 hours per week.

questions.question: A production-ready custom agent stack should take 3-6 months to build properly.
questions.explanation: False. The lesson prescribes a 7-14 day implementation timeline for a production-ready stack, maintained in 2-3 hours per week. If it takes longer than 14 days, you're over-engineering. Ship a working v1, then iterate based on real usage data.

questions.question: Using the architecture decision tree from the lesson, determine your agent stack path (self-hosted, hybrid, or SaaS) based on your budget and technical comfort. List the specific tools for each agent you plan to deploy and estimate the total monthly cost.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response follows the decision tree logic (budget tier + technical comfort = architecture path), lists specific tools with costs for each agent, and arrives at a realistic total monthly cost. Look for understanding that the stack should be buildable in 7-14 days, not over-engineered.
